top_signals.title: Hegseth Hails China Relationship at Defense Summit
top_signals.summary:
Jabs at European allies and talks of stable US-China ties — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth took to the stage at a security summit in Singapore this weekend. 

But two topics were notably missing from his address: Taiwan and the Iran war. Haslinda Amin with more. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.summary: Oil-linked trading on unregulated and decentralised crypto exchange has skyrocketed during Iran war

top_signals.title: China Pledges to Retaliate If EU Imposes Trade Restrictions
top_signals.summary: China warned it would “resolutely” retaliate if the European Union proceeds with new restrictive trade measures, following a May 29 European Commission discussion on China policy.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin retail sentiment still matters, says Swan Bitcoin CEO
top_signals.summary: Swan Bitcoin CEO Cory Klippsten said it is important to pay attention to retail sentiment around Bitcoin, as ownership is still far from concentrated and “it’s not like Blackrock owns the Bitcoin.”
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: US Drillers Add More Rigs In Response to Higher Prices
top_signals.summary: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States rose this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 562,  down just 1 from this same time last year. The number of active oil rigs rose by 4 to 429 during the latest reporting period, according to the data. This is 22 below this same time last year. The number of gas rigs stayed the same at 125, which is 16 more than this time last year. The miscellaneous rig stayed the same at 8. The latest EIA data…

top_signals.title: Japan used record $73.6 billion to support yen over past month
top_signals.summary: Finance Ministry data for the month from April 28 to Wednesday showed a total intervention of ¥11.73 trillion ($73.6 billion) over a period marked by several spikes in the yen.

top_signals.title: ‘Extraordinarily unusual’ for CFTC to reverse Gemini settlement deal: Ex-chair
top_signals.summary: As the CFTC and Gemini work together to seek a court's reversal of a 2025 settlement, one of the agency’s former chairs said the public “deserves a better explanation.”
